description_long: This is a sleek, silver-gray military jet airplane with a sharply pointed nose and a long, slender fuselage. The aircraft features short, trapezoidal wings mounted low on the body, and a prominent vertical stabilizer at the tail, accompanied by two horizontal stabilizers. Markings on the fuselage include the insignia of the United States Air Force and a star-and-bar roundel, indicating its military affiliation. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a streamlined canopy. The landing gear is visible, with a single nose wheel and a main gear set further back. The overall design is highly aerodynamic, optimized for high-speed flight.
description: A silver-gray military jet with a pointed nose, low-mounted short wings, and prominent US Air Force markings. Features an enclosed forward cockpit and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, pointed nose, low-mounted wings, and US Air Force markings along the fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the sharp nose, narrow fuselage, and the symmetrical placement of the wings and tail surfaces.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same elongated profile, wing configuration, and tail assembly as the first side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the twin horizontal stabilizers, vertical stabilizer, and the streamlined tail section.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with short, straight, trapezoidal wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single rear-mounted jet engine integrated into the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers in a standard arrangement.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with a single nose wheel and two main gear units, likely retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a streamlined canopy, likely single-seat.
